sections.title: Our Diversity, Accessibility, Inclusion And Respect (DAIR) Policy And Statement
sections.description:
At Accessibility Innovations, we are committed to the idea that everyone has the right to full participation in our society. This includes access to employment standards, customer-service standards, and barrier-free public spaces! Our DAIR policy reflects our commitment to creating an environment where everyone feels valued. With our equitable practices and proactive initiatives, we ensure to create a culture that embraces individuality, is inclusive, and empowers everyone to thrive.

Our leadership is committed and accountable to our Diversity, Accessibility, Inclusion and Respect (DAIR) policy and statement, which are established to ensure a fully inclusive and respectful workplace environment. In DAIR, we stand for the following:

items.title: Diversity
items.description: Our diverse identities, experiences, and qualities shape our perspectives and interactions with the world. These encompass factors like race, ethnicity, gender, age, abilities, beliefs, and personal attributes, influencing how we engage and are perceived by others.

items.title: Accessibility
items.description: Ensuring that people of all abilities have equitable and barrier-free access to physical spaces, products, programs, and services, as well as employment opportunities.

items.title: Inclusion
items.description: Recognizing, welcoming, and making positive space for diversity. An inclusive workplace environment capitalizes on the diversity of thoughts, experiences, skills, and talents of all of our colleagues.

items.title: Respect
items.description: A respectful environment values diversity, inclusion, dignity, fairness, positive communication, and professionalism. It is inclusive, equitable, accessible, and free from harassment and discrimination.

image.alt: An illustration showing people of various abilities standing beside a large staircase with a label on each step. Going up the staircase, the labels are Diversity, Accessibility, Inclusion, Respect, and D A I R.

sections.title: what our clients have to say

image.alt: FSRA Financial Services Regulatory Authority of Ontario

sections.quote: SilverRide allowed us to maintain a leadership position in providing our valued clients with fully transparent and consistent communications, within the proper regulatory Accessibility frameworks and conformance standards. They have been responsible for making all public communications fully accessible since 2016.
sections.company: Financial Services Regulatory Authority of Ontario

sections.title: We Care
sections.description:
The World Health Organization estimates that nineteen million children below the age of fifteen are visually impaired. Twelve million of these children are visually impaired due to refractive errors. This condition is easily diagnosed and corrected with eye surgery performed before age 12 to prevent permanent blindness.

SilverRide Canada is proud to collaborate with L V Prasad Eye Institute, India, to fund programs aimed at making high-quality, appropriate eye care accessible to all. Watch this section of our website for further developments on this exciting initiative.
